raw_consumer_statement:
Complaint Summary Barclays Bank Delaware XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  ) On XX/XX/year>, I received both a text and an email from Barclays Bank alerting me to suspicious activity on my XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ending in XXXX. I immediately called Barclays and spoke with a representative named XXXX. She informed me that a new card had recently been requested in my name, which I never authorized. I confirmed that I did not make a {$160.00} purchase at XXXX XXXX in XXXX, NY, nor any cash advances or purchases in that area. I also immediately locked all my Barclays cards online. 
Barclays issued me a replacement card via XXXX  and opened a fraud investigation. However, I later received a letter stating that Barclays determined the disputed transaction was valid because the card was identified as being in your possession and chip card technology was used. Based on this conclusion, Barclays reversed the provisional credit and reapplied the charge to my account. 
I strongly dispute this decision. I was in possession of my original card the entire time and never authorized or participated in these transactions. The claim that chip technology can not be compromised is incorrectthere are numerous cases of chip card fraud and cloning. Barclays determination fails to provide any proof of my authorization, such as signature verification, video evidence, or merchant confirmation. 
I am requesting that Barclays reopen and reinvestigate this case under the Truth in Lending Act and Regulation Z ( 12 CFR 1026.13 ), which requires proper investigation and consumer protection against unauthorized use. I also request copies of all documentation Barclays relied upon to conclude that the transaction was valid, including any merchant logs or chip transaction data. I was also informed to submit via fax documentation that proved my existence, namely drivers license, utility bill and a copy of my social security card to fax number XXXX. I also included specific instructions on corresponding and confirming receipt, which I never received. 
This situation has caused me financial harm and unnecessary distress. Barclays investigation appears inadequate and dismissive of clear evidence that my account was compromised. I am seeking restoration of the provisional credit and full removal of the disputed charge, along with written confirmation that my fraud report is being properly reviewed.
